title: THEO OF GOLDEN
author: Allen Levi
description: A man travels to a small Southern town, where he purchases pencil drawings of local residents and exchanges them for stories.

title: PROJECT HAIL MARY
author: Andy Weir
description: Ryland Grace awakes from a long sleep alone and far from home, and the fate of humanity rests on his shoulders; the basis of the movie.

title: YESTERYEAR
author: Caro Claire Burke
description: Natalie Heller Mills, a privileged tradwife social media influencer, wakes up to find she is living in the comparatively difficult reality of 1855.

title: PURPLE STATE
author: Dana Perino
description: A public relations professional goes with two of her friends to a swing district in Wisconsin and meets a guy with a different perspective.

title: THE CORRESPONDENT
author: Virginia Evans
description: Letters from someone she used to know push Sybil Van Antwerp toward revisiting her past and finding a way to forgive.

title: STRANGERS
author: Belle Burden
description: Burden retraces her marriage of 20 years in search of clues to help shape her understanding about its demise and to find a way forward.

title: WHEN WE SEE YOU AGAIN
author: Rachel Goldberg-Polin
description: A mother recounts the capture of her son, Hersh, from the Nova Music Festival and his subsequent execution.

title: FAMESICK
author: Lena Dunham
description: The author of “Not That Kind of Girl” evaluates the effects that pursuing her creative endeavors had on her.

title: LONDON FALLING
author: Patrick Radden Keefe
description: The author of “Say Nothing” details the efforts by the parents of a 19-year-old Londoner to uncover the truth about his mysterious death and secret life.

title: THE BODY KEEPS THE SCORE
author: Bessel van der Kolk
description: How trauma affects the body and mind, and innovative treatments for recovery.

title: WONDER
author: R.J. Palacio
description: A boy with a facial deformity starts school. (Ages 8 to 12)

title: KPOP DEMON HUNTERS
author: Jessica Yoon
description: Huntrix takes on the Saja Boys, a boy band who happen to be demons in disguise. Based on the movie. (Ages 7 to 10)

title: THE COMPLETE COOKBOOK FOR YOUNG CHEFS
author: America's Test Kitchen Kids
description: Over 100 kid-tested recipes from America's Test Kitchen. (Ages 8 and up)

title: FORBIDDEN MOUNTAIN
author: Brandon Mull
description: Two gifted teens must prevent an ancient evil from re-entering their world. (Ages 8 to 12)

title: UNSETTLING SALAD!
author: Aaron Reynolds. Illustrated Peter Brown
description: Thadeus and Oliver take a bite out of a salad that may bite back! (Ages 6 to 9)

title: WHY A SON NEEDS A MOM
author: Gregory E. Lang. Illustrated Gail Yerrill
description: The special bond between mother and son. (Ages 4 to 7)

title: MEI MEI THE BUNNY
author: Laufey. Illustrated Lauren O’Hara
description: Mei Mei tries to shake the butterflies of performing in her first recital. (Ages 3 to 7)

title: IT'S MY BIRD-DAY!
author: Mo Willems
description: The pigeon celebrates his birthday, but he’s not the only one. (Ages 3 to 5)

title: BECAUSE I HAD A TEACHER
author: Kobi Yamada. Illustrated Natalie Russell
description: An ode to teachers. (Ages 4 to 7)

title: FOR THE FANS!
author: Angela Song
description: Meet the members of the K-pop group Huntrix. (Ages 2 to 5)

title: THE HUNGER GAMES
author: Suzanne Collins
description: In a dystopia, a girl fights for survival on live TV. (Ages 12 and up)

title: DIARY OF A WIMPY KID
author: Jeff Kinney. Illustrated Jeff Kinney
description: The travails and challenges of adolescence. (Ages 9 to 12)

title: WINGS OF FIRE
author: Tui T. Sutherland
description: Only the five dragonets of destiny can unite the seven warring dragon tribes. (Ages 9 to 12)

title: HARRY POTTER
author: J.K. Rowling
description: A wizard hones his conjuring skills in the service of fighting evil. (Ages 10 and up)

title: BOYS OF TOMMEN
author: Chloe Walsh
description: In Ireland, friends at the private school Tommen College prepare for adulthood. (Ages 16 to 18)

title: RELEASE ME
author: Tahereh Mafi
description: The romantic tension mounts between Rosabelle and James as the fight in the New Republic continues. (Ages 14 and up)

title: THE ESCAPE GAME
author: Marissa Meyer and Tamara Moss
description: A real murder takes place on an escape room-themed game show. (Ages 14 and up)

title: THE THORN QUEEN
author: Sasha Peyton Smith
description: Ivy Benton, now the queen of England, schemes to dethrone her husband, save her sister and reunite with her true love, Emmett. (Ages 13 and up)

title: FAKE SKATING
author: Lynn Painter
description: After years of separation, the childhood friends Alec and Dani reunite and face their true feelings toward each other. (Ages 14 and up)

title: THE WAY THINGS WORK: NEWLY REVISED EDITION
author: David Macaulay
description: A look at how technology impacts our lives. (Ages 11 to 15)

title: THE LET THEM THEORY
author: Mel Robbins
description: Read by the author. 10 hours, 38 minutes unabridged.

title: ATOMIC HABITS
author: James Clear
description: Read by the author. 5 hours, 35 minutes unabridged.

title: THE 48 LAWS OF POWER
author: Robert Greene
description: Read by Richard Poe. 23 hours, 6 minutes unabridged.

title: THE KING IS COMING
author: John Bevere
description: Read by the author. 9 hours, 45 minutes unabridged.

title: NEVER SPLIT THE DIFFERENCE
author: Chris Voss with Tahl Raz
description: Read by Michael Kramer. 8 hours, 7 minutes unabridged.
